page: Microsoft Azure D64ds_v7 is a general-purpose virtual machine instance belonging to the Ddsv7 family, running on the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor. It features 64 dedicated vCPUs mapped to 32 physical cores of an Intel Xeon 6973P-C processor using the x86_64 architecture. The system is equipped with 256.0 GB of system memory, providing a balanced ratio of 4.0 GB of RAM per core. For local storage, it includes 3780 GB of local disk capacity and supports Premium Storage. No GPUs are included in this configuration.

The underlying Intel Xeon processor features a substantial L3 cache of 480 MB, alongside 2 MB of L2 cache and 48 KB of L1d cache. It supports advanced instruction sets including Advanced Vector Extensions (AVX, AVX512F, AVX512_VNNI, AVX_VNNI, AVX512_BF16) and Advanced Matrix Extensions (AMX_TILE, AMX_INT8). Cryptographic acceleration is supported via AES, VAES, and SHA_NI flags, while integer and bit manipulation are handled by BMI2, ADX, and FMA.

Benchmark data indicates strong multi-core performance, with stress-ng multi-core CPU and PassMark CPU scores placing in the top 25% and top 10% respectively. Single-core performance is moderate, with stress-ng single-core scoring in the middle 50%. Memory bandwidth is consistently strong, reaching 326.02 GB/sec for large block read-only operations and 127.56 GB/sec for large block write operations. The instance demonstrates top-tier capabilities in single-threaded gzip compression (36.06 GB/sec) and static web serving throughput (104.9 GB/sec). For lightweight AI workloads, prompt processing with a 135M model achieves a speed of 3946.15 tokens/sec.

From a cost-efficiency perspective, the D64ds_v7 offers a dense allocation of dedicated compute resources and high-capacity local storage, making it suitable for workloads requiring local scratch space without the overhead of external storage attachments. However, the average single-core CPU and memory benchmark scores represent a tradeoff for workloads that rely heavily on single-threaded execution speed. This instance is configured for database operations, redis caching, static web serving, and multi-threaded data processing tasks.
description: Microsoft Azure D64ds_v7 is a general-purpose virtual machine from the Ddsv7 family, powered by an Intel Xeon 6973P-C processor. It features 64 dedicated vCPUs (32 physical cores), 256.0 GB of RAM, and 3780 GB of local storage. Operating on the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor, this x86_64 instance includes advanced CPU instruction sets such as AVX-512 and AMX. Benchmarks show strong multi-core CPU performance, high memory bandwidth, and top-tier static web serving throughput. While single-core performance remains average, the instance excels in multi-threaded tasks, showing strong results in database operations and Redis SET operations. This hardware profile is suited for database hosting, web serving, and multi-threaded data processing workloads requiring substantial local storage.
og_description: Microsoft Azure D64ds_v7 is a general-purpose instance featuring 64 vCPUs, 256 GB RAM, and 3780 GB local storage, delivering strong multi-core and database performance for enterprise workloads.
meta_description: Microsoft Azure D64ds_v7 features 64 Intel Xeon vCPUs, 256 GB RAM, and 3780 GB local storage, optimized for database and web serving workloads.
tagline: A balanced x86_64 virtual machine combining 64 dedicated vCPUs, 256 GB RAM, and high-capacity local storage for demanding multi-threaded workloads.
bullet_points: 64 dedicated Intel Xeon vCPUs and 256 GB of system memory
3780 GB of local storage with Premium Storage capability
Advanced instruction support including AVX-512 and AMX
Strong multi-core CPU and database benchmark performance
Top-tier static web serving and single-threaded compression speeds
Suited for database hosting, caching, and web serving
